case_number: Constitutional Petition is dismissed as not

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P. No.D-5686 of 2023 ___________________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature of Judge ___________________________________________________________________ FRESH CASE: 1. For order on office objection. 2. For hearing of main case. ----------- Dated; 6th December 2023 Malik Altaf Hussain, Advocate has shown appearance and files vakalatnama on behalf of the petitioner, which is taken on record. While confronted as to maintainability of instant petition in view of Order dated 27.11.2023 passed by this Court in the instant petition, learned counsel for the petitioner submits that petitioner will not press instant petition and will pursue the departmental remedy, which has already been availed by the petitioner by filing representation before the Secretary, Revenue Department, Government of Sindh, Hyderabad, however, requests that directions may be issued to the concerned officer to decide the appeal of the petitioner at an early date in accordance with law after providing an opportunity of being heard. Accordingly, instant Constitutional Petition is dismissed as not pressed alongwith listed application. However, the petitioner will be at liberty to pursue the departmental remedy, which according to learned counsel, has already availed in the shape of representation made before the Secretary, Revenue Department, Government of Sindh, Hyderabad. It is, however, expected that if, such appeal is not decided so far, same shall be decided within a period of four weeks from the date of receipt of this order, which shall be communicated by the petitioner to the concerned authority, in accordance with law.

However, the petitioner will be at liberty to pursue the departmental remedy, which according to learned counsel, has already availed in the shape of representation made before the Secretary, Revenue Department, Government of Sindh, Hyderabad. It is, however, expected that if, such appeal is not decided so far, same shall be decided within a period of four weeks from the date of receipt of this order, which shall be communicated by the petitioner to the concerned authority, in accordance with law. ACTING CHIEF JUSTICE J U D G E *Farhan/PS*
